title: FR-36 Formal Reasoning Battery Item
description: Schema for one question in the FR-36 (Formal Reasoning) battery. Each item tests graph-structural reasoning capability against a being's Y2 InferenceRule layer. Three levels: FR1 (single-step inference), FR2 (multi-step chain), FR-boundary (correct refusal of formal-method tasks).

id.description: Stable question identifier matching the source filename stem.

level.description: FR1: basic inference (single Y2 rule). FR2: multi-step chain (2-3 hops). FR-boundary: appropriate refusal of formal-method tasks.

subtype.description: Sub-classification: for FR1, the InferenceRule type (IMPLICATION|CONSTRAINT|SUBSUMPTION|EQUIVALENCE|EXCLUSION|INFERENCE|COMPOSITION). For FR2, the chain depth (e.g., '2-hop'). For FR-boundary, the formal method required (e.g., 'algebraic-derivation').

rule_chain.description: FR2 only: arrow-separated rule types in the chain (e.g., 'IMPLICATION → SUBSUMPTION').

expected_behavior.description: answer: the being should produce the inferred conclusion. refuse: the being should decline because the task requires formal-mathematical machinery beyond graph capability.

expected_behavior_detail.description: Full PASS / FAIL criteria from the source markdown.

graph_eval_criteria.description: Graph-structural evaluation criteria (Y2 rules, predicates, confidence thresholds).

target_level.description: Target being-level for which this question is calibrated.
